target: WIN-053 detail_evidence in data/wins.json — targeted single-sentence replacement of the EXP-219 third-horn closure sentence. Two moderate c8 holes addressed in one edit: (A) change "parameterless surface fit" to "three-parameter surface fit — with none of A, C, or λ derived from dome geometry" so the phrase is literally accurate (B(r) has three explicit parameters: A, C, and λ); (B) soften "the toroidal geometry does no predictive work" to "the toroidal geometry does no numerical predictive work beyond dictating that the fit must have two peaks" so the clause survives adversarial quotation. No other paragraph, sentence, field, TLDR, verdict, or code_analysis is altered. The §6.1 cross-reference anchor (#p6-61-0) is preserved unchanged.
source_issue: ISS-1410 (moderate H1), ISS-1411 (moderate H2) — both on the EXP-219 third-horn closure sentence
patch_instructions: One edit to WIN-053 detail_evidence in data/wins.json. Find the unique substring patch_anchors[0].before (verified 1 occurrence 2026-04-22) and replace with patch_anchors[0].after. No other fields are touched. TLDRs remain unchanged (they do not surface the third-horn closure). After integration, verify the dashboard and index renderers still produce clean prose and that the <a href="#p6-61-0"> cross-reference still resolves.

anchor.before: A third retreat — claiming the toroidal flow is aetheric rather than Maxwellian, so ∇·B = 0 does not apply and flux conservation is simply inapplicable — is the defender's strongest remaining move, but it abandons the very claim WIN-053 rests on. Once interior flux conservation is waived, no derivation links A, C, or λ to the toroidal geometry: B(r) demotes to a parameterless surface fit to observed pole magnitudes (the "observed values relabeled" failure mode documented in §6.1), the dome's 20% RMS "improvement" is no longer a derivation at all but a curve fit, and the toroidal geometry does no predictive work.
anchor.after: A third retreat — claiming the toroidal flow is aetheric rather than Maxwellian, so ∇·B = 0 does not apply and flux conservation is simply inapplicable — is the defender's strongest remaining move, but it abandons the very claim WIN-053 rests on. Once interior flux conservation is waived, no derivation links A, C, or λ to the toroidal geometry: B(r) demotes to a three-parameter surface fit to observed pole magnitudes — with none of A, C, or λ derived from dome geometry (the "observed values relabeled" failure mode documented in §6.1) — the dome's 20% RMS "improvement" is no longer a derivation at all but a curve fit, and the toroidal geometry does no numerical predictive work beyond dictating that the fit must have two peaks.

patch_anchors.after: A third retreat — claiming the toroidal flow is aetheric rather than Maxwellian, so ∇·B = 0 does not apply and flux conservation is simply inapplicable — is the defender's strongest remaining move, but it abandons the very claim WIN-053 rests on. Once interior flux conservation is waived, no derivation links A, C, or λ to the toroidal geometry: B(r) demotes to a three-parameter surface fit to observed pole magnitudes — with none of A, C, or λ derived from dome geometry (the "observed values relabeled" failure mode documented in §6.1) — the dome's 20% RMS "improvement" is no longer a derivation at all but a curve fit, and the toroidal geometry does no numerical predictive work beyond dictating that the fit must have two peaks.
patch_anchors.rationale: c8 H1 (ISS-1410) and c8 H2 (ISS-1411) in a single atomic edit. H1: "parameterless surface fit" is self-contradictory with paragraph 4 two paragraphs later, which explicitly names A=62,376, C=64,852, and λ=8,619 as fitted coefficients. A sharp defender quoting this sentence out of context could ridicule the review for calling a three-parameter equation "parameterless." The curmudgeon's recommendation option (a) is adopted verbatim: "three-parameter surface fit to observed pole magnitudes — none of A, C, or λ derived from dome geometry." H2: "the toroidal geometry does no predictive work" over-reaches. A defender can respond that the toroidal architecture predicts a two-peak B(r) (one term per pole) rather than the single monopolar e^(−r/λ) of V50.6 — a functional-form prediction, however weak. The softening "does no numerical predictive work beyond dictating that the fit must have two peaks" preserves the rhetorical closure while being defensible under adversarial quotation (the curmudgeon's explicit recommendation). The §6.1 cross-reference anchor #p6-61-0 is preserved verbatim.

holes_addressed.description: "Parameterless surface fit" is technically imprecise. B(r) = A·e^(−r_N/λ) + C·e^(−r_S/λ) has three explicit parameters (A=62,376 nT, C=64,852 nT, λ=8,619 km), all three literally present in the equation. The intended meaning is "no parameters derived from dome geometry" (parameters are fitted rather than derived), but the literal phrase "parameterless" is self-contradictory with paragraph 4 which explicitly names A, C, and λ as fitted coefficients.
holes_addressed.fix: Phrase changed to "three-parameter surface fit to observed pole magnitudes — with none of A, C, or λ derived from dome geometry." Literally accurate and preserves the rhetorical force of the original.

holes_addressed.description: "The toroidal geometry does no predictive work" is over-strong. A defender can respond that the toroidal architecture predicts a sum-of-two-terms functional form (one per pole) versus the single monopolar e^(−r/λ) of V50.6 — a functional-form prediction, however weak and however entangled with unfalsifiable aether-to-matter coupling. The preceding clause "no derivation links A, C, or λ to the toroidal geometry" already carries the correct narrower point; the final clause overshoots.
holes_addressed.fix: Clause softened to "does no numerical predictive work beyond dictating that the fit must have two peaks." Preserves rhetorical closure while being defensible under adversarial quotation. The curmudgeon's explicit recommendation option.

new_evidence_added: No new evidence added; this is a precision-of-language fix to an existing third-horn-closure sentence. Both changes are within the scope of the original EXP-219 claim (the aetheric retreat destroys the dome's derivation).
The phrase "none of A, C, or λ derived from dome geometry" makes the paragraph internally consistent with paragraph 4 (which names A, C, λ as fitted coefficients) instead of contradicting it.

anticipated_objections.objection: The softened "does no numerical predictive work beyond dictating that the fit must have two peaks" still concedes a predictive contribution, making the claim less strong overall.
anticipated_objections.response: Yes — that is the point. Conceding the weak prediction (two peaks) is honest: the toroidal architecture does require two terms in the fit, one per pole. The strong claim (no numerical predictive work at all) is false and gives a defender an opening. Ceding the weak prediction protects the strong claim that all three parameters are fitted, none derived from dome geometry. The c8 curmudgeon's recommendation balances defensibility against rhetorical force and concludes the softening is net-positive.

anticipated_objections.objection: The "three-parameter" phrasing makes it sound like three parameters is a lot, potentially framing B(r) as more empirically supported than the review intends.
anticipated_objections.response: The added qualifier — "with none of A, C, or λ derived from dome geometry" — prevents this reading. A three-parameter fit where none of the parameters come from the underlying model is a curve fit, not a derivation. The §6.1 cross-reference reinforces that framing. The reader cannot come away thinking three fitted-to-data parameters strengthens the dome's case.

kernel_of_truth: Both moderate holes are valid. ISS-1410 is a clear factual imprecision — "parameterless" literally contradicts paragraph 4's enumeration of A, C, λ. ISS-1411 is a rhetorical over-reach — "no predictive work" ignores the minimal functional-form contribution of the toroidal topology (two terms vs. one). The original EXP-219 sentence was net-positive (closing the third horn was more important than minor imprecisions in its closing clauses), and the c8 curmudgeon explicitly flagged both as "not grounds for rollback, single-sentence edit, low risk." Adopting both recommendations simultaneously in one atomic patch is the least-invasive fix.
cross_references: EXP-219 (integrated in c8) — this patch refines the sentence EXP-219 inserted. EXP-219 was correct in substance and location; the refinement is precision-of-language only.

WIN-053 paragraph 4 (detail_evidence) — explicitly names A=62,376, C=64,852, λ=8,619 as fitted coefficients. The new phrasing "three-parameter surface fit" is internally consistent with this enumeration; the old phrasing "parameterless" was not.

Section 6.1 "Zero Fitted Parameters — The Hidden Parameters" — the §6.1 cross-reference anchor #p6-61-0 is preserved in the new sentence. The #p6-61-0 anchor was verified by the c8 curmudgeon review against the rendered docs/index.html.
tldr_verdict of WIN-053 — already uses #p6-61-0. The main-text and TLDR forms remain consistent after this patch.

notes_for_decider: Single-sentence replacement in WIN-053 detail_evidence. Anchor verified unique on 2026-04-22. The #p6-61-0 anchor is preserved verbatim (including the showTab('predictions') onclick). TLDRs (tldr_evidence, tldr_verdict) are intentionally unchanged — they already foreground the flux-conservation argument and the §6.1 cross-reference; neither TLDR invokes the third-horn closure, so neither needs updating. After integration: (a) verify detail_evidence still renders cleanly, (b) verify the §6.1 link resolves, (c) run tests for no new failures. Character delta on detail_evidence: +149 (3,676 → 3,825 for the replaced sentence; field total ~8,126 → ~8,275).
deeper_analysis:
Both holes are small but genuine. The "parameterless" imprecision is the more obviously fixable one — it is literally self-contradictory with paragraph 4 and a defender could screenshot the contradiction. The "no predictive work" over-reach is subtler but more important rhetorically: adversarial defenders routinely extract sweeping claims from reviews and show them to be over-strong, which damages reviewer credibility more than the underlying argument damage. The c8 curmudgeon's recommendation explicitly balances "rhetorical closure" against "defensibility under adversarial quotation" and concludes the softening is net-positive. I agree — the softened version is actually stronger because it concedes a minimal truth (two peaks) to protect the load-bearing claim (none of A, C, λ derived from geometry).

I considered handling H1 and H2 as two separate EXPs to allow finer-grained revert history, but the two edits are in the same sentence and share context; splitting would create a confusing integration sequence ("edit sentence S to S'", then immediately "edit S' to S''") with no benefit. A single atomic patch is cleaner.

On the other side of the argument (user preference, applied):

1. A defender could argue that "three-parameter" normalizes B(r) as an empirical fit and implicitly concedes it is empirically supported. Counter: the added qualifier "with none of A, C, or λ derived from dome geometry" prevents that reading. Without the qualifier, "three-parameter" alone could be mistaken for an endorsement; with the qualifier, it is a precise statement of the failure mode. The phrasing mirrors standard terminology in model-selection literature (e.g., "three-parameter free fit" is a common neutral description).

2. A defender could argue the softened "two peaks" concession gives up ground the review already won. Counter: the ground was not won — a defender can always point to paragraph 4 of WIN-053 itself, which acknowledges that A and C are two separate coefficients (one per pole), to claim the two-peak structure is predicted. Conceding this explicitly, in controlled language, is more honest than pretending the weak prediction does not exist.

3. A defender could argue the softening signals reviewer over-sensitivity to adversarial quotation. Counter: EXP-219's deeper_analysis explicitly noted that ranking defender moves ("strongest remaining move") is the right posture, because credit foregrounds closure. The same discipline applies here: conceding weak predictions openly is a kernel-of-truth move that strengthens the load-bearing claim.

4. Citation stability: #p6-61-0 is the correct §6.1 anchor (verified in c8 citation_check); the new sentence preserves it verbatim along with the showTab('predictions') onclick. No citation drift.
tldr_change: None. tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict of WIN-053 already foreground the flux-conservation argument and the §6.1 cross-reference. Neither TLDR invokes the third-horn closure, "parameterless" phrasing, or "no predictive work" phrasing — the sentence-level precision fix is entirely in detail_evidence. Per analyst.md TLDR review standard ("Fix factual errors. Do not split hairs on nuance — the expanded detail handles that"), the TLDRs are correct as-is.
